Football.title: Chelsea icon Thiago Silva flies back to Brazil and shares heartbreaking message after tragic death of his mum
Football.content:
CHELSEA legend Thiago Silva flew back to Brazil following the heartbreaking death of his mother.

Silva, 41, took to social media on Sunday to reveal the sad news.





The Brazilian defender posted a series of photos including selfies with his mum Angela.

Other pictures appeared to be of her friends and family mourning her tragic passing at the funeral in his homeland.

Silva paid an emotional tribute to his doting mother with a heartwrenching message.

He wrote: “Rest in peace, my mother!

“I will miss you so much.

“Watch over us from above.

“I will love you forever my crown.”

Various team-mates from throughout his career replied with messages of support, including Willian, Felipe Melo, Marco Verratti and Chelsea captain Reece James.



The Stamford Bridge club also took to X with a classy post.

The club typed: “Everyone at Chelsea FC is deeply saddened to learn of the passing of Angela Maria da Silva, beloved mother of Thiago Silva.

“Our thoughts are with Thiago, and all of Angela’s family and friends at this difficult time.”

Silva spent four years at Chelsea after shining for AC Milan and PSG.

He was part of Thomas Tuchel’s team that won the Champions League in 2021.



The 113-cap Brazil icon left in 2024 for Fluminense but is now back in Europe after joining Porto in December, making his debut the following month.

His appearance, starting in a 1-0 cup victory over rivals Benfica, saw him overtake Pepe as Porto’s oldest player.

Football.title: Michael Carrick speaks out on Bruno Fernandes’ Man Utd future amid Italy and Portugal transfer interest
Football.content:
MANCHESTER UNITED coach Michael Carrick insists Bruno Fernandes is “not someone we’d want to lose” nine months after the club were willing to sell him.

United were prepared to let Fernandes move to Saudi Pro League side Al Nassr for £80million until former coach Ruben Amorim intervened and pleaded with Fernandes to stay.





Fernandes, 31, broke David Beckham‘s club record of 15 Premier League assists in the 3-1 win against Aston Villa, with his pass for Matheus Cunha‘s winner his 100th assist for United.

The club captain is under contract until next year, with the option of an additional year, and Fernandes has spoken about the appeal of possibly returning to Portugal or Italy to finish his career.

Carrick has said he is planning for the long-term at United and indicated he would be opposed to Fernandes departing before his contract expires.

“In terms of the club and moving forward, it’s difficult for me to get involved in too much of that,” Carrick said.

“Bruno‘s definitely not someone we’d want to lose, I can say that.

“But the summer and beyond that, it’s difficult for me to kind of go too far with that.

“But certainly, he’s important for us and he’s definitely not one we would want to lose.

“I think he’s important for us and he’s playing in in positions now where we we need to find him and we need to we need to.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

“Obviously, supply him as much as we can in for order time to to to make the difference, but it’s not just about Bruno.

“I understand that he’s had some really important moments recently and a long time he’s been here.

“But I think it’s a team and then the importance of of sharing that responsibility, so it’s not always on one player, I think is really important.

“And then it does make everyone else’s job that little bit easier, so he’s not taking everything on himself. Which he shouldn’t, and he doesn’t really feel for us.”

Carrick revealed that first team coach Jonny Evans has worked with Fernandes and Casemiro on set pieces.



Casemiro converted Fernandes’ first-half corner for the fifth goal that combination has produced this season and the Brazilian’s six goals are the most from dead-ball kicks.

Carrick added: “He (Fernandes)’s done it for a long period of time now in the big moments and making the difference.

“And whether he’s scoring or creating he’s normally around it.

“Two very different goals. Obviously today, I think he’s got a really good understanding of Case and working with Johnny on the set plays and different things and spaces and what not.

“And there’s also the bit you can coach in them. There’s a little bit of the plays and understanding that they’ve got a great connection there with that.



“And they’ve done it a few times before, so that was good. And then obviously the pass (for Cunha), getting in positions where you can play that final pass and it was one we’ve seen before.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“And then he plays Ben (Sesko) in as well after that.

“There’s not a lot else to say that I’ve sat here and said before about Bruno, but I thought he was really good.”

Football.title: Captain of Iranian women’s football team leaves Australia after asylum bid as team’s ‘family members are missing’
Football.content:
REGIME'S LEVERAGE Captain of Iranian women’s football team leaves Australia after asylum bid as team’s ‘family members are missing’

THE captain of the Iranian women’s football team has withdrawn her chance at sanctuary in Australia as fears grow for players’ family members.

Zahra Ghanbari, 34, is the fifth member of the squad to backtrack her decision to seek asylum in Australia.





She will fly back to Iran via Malaysia, the IRNA news agency reported on Sunday.

The captain joins Zahra Soltan Meshkehkar, Mona Hamoudi, and Zahra Sarbali, who all also reversed their asylum bid on Saturday.

On Wednesday, the first player to change her mind sparked fears that threats were being made to family members back in Iran.

Shiva Amini, an exiled former Iranian national futsal player, confirmed those fears on Sunday.

Follow The Sun’s liveblog for the latest updates on Iran

“Several of the players decided to go back because the threats against their families became unbearable and the intimidation was relentless,” she wrote on X.

She said she had received information that Iran’s Football Federation was working with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps to place “intense and systemic pressure” on the players’ families in Iran.



Just two of the original seven squad members remain in Australia after escaping their handlers with assistance from police.

The government agreed to provide humanitarian visas for the six players and one staff member in hiding.

Iranian media reports praised Ghanbari’s decision to return home, while also mocking the efforts of both Australia and America to grant the women asylum.

State-run outlet IRNA wrote that Ghanbari was “returning to the embrace of the homeland”, while the Mehr news agency called it a “patriotic decision”.

In an brave act of defiance, the Iranian women’s football team were silent for the country’s national anthem in their opening Asia Cup match against South Korea on March 2.



This led to them being vilified back home in Iran – labelled “war traitors” in the media.

One player reportedly received a chilling voice note from her mother saying: “Don’t come [back to Iran]… they’ll kill you.”

Another message smuggled by a player’s family out of Iran to the squad read: “You need to stay”.



President Donald Trump also warned the team “will most likely be killed” if they were allowed to return to Iran.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Iran’s Sports Ministry has since publicly welcomed the players’ decision.

“The national spirit and patriotism of the Iranian women’s national football team defeated the enemy’s plans against this team,” the Ministry said in a statement.

Football.title: Liverpool 1 Tottenham 1: Richarlison trolls Anfield crowd with late equaliser to end Spurs’ brutal losing run
Football.content:
RICHARLISON saved a point, saved his boss and just may well have provided the spark to save Spurs from the drop.

The Brazilian scuffed home in the 90th minute to chalk off Dominik Szoboszlai’s early free-kick.





It spared Guglielmo Vicario’s blushes, after he became the latest Tottenham keeper to have a cock-up this week when failing to stop Szoboszlai’s dead-ball effort.

It will most likely keep interim chief Igor Tudor in his job, after widespread reports that his interim spell in charge could be cut short if this had been a fifth straight defeat for the Croat.

Instead, Spurs grabbed the most precious of points in the unlikeliest of places, given their horrendous form and history at Anfield.

The draw pushed them one clear of relegation rivals Nottingham Forest and West Ham, and moved them to within two of Leeds.

And while it by no means gets them out of danger – their winless league streak was actually extended to 12 matches here – it could provide the launchpad from which they secure their survival.

It was the first point of Tudor’s short-term reign, which had hit a new low on Tuesday with the 5-2 battering at Atletico Madrid.



That game saw Vicario dropped and replaced by Antonin Kinksy, but the Czech lasted only 17 minutes after being hauled off following two shocking gaffes.

That Champions League last-16 clash at the Metropolitano – the scene of Spurs’ final defeat to Liverpool in the competition in 2019 – was so catastrophic, it was hard to believe.

CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

The goalkeeper shambles rightly grabbed the headlines, but arguably more damaging was the clash of heads right at the end of the game between captain Cristian Romero and Joao Palhinha.

It ended up ruling the influential pair out of this fixture, taking Tudor’s absentee list to 12 for this fixture, what with Micky van de Ven suspended and 11 out with injury.

That figure rose to an unlucky 13 when Conor Gallagher failed to recover from sickness, despite making the trip to Anfield.



It led to Tudor playing rookie left-back Souza as a right winger in a surprise 4-4-2 formation.

While Arne Slot opted to start Mo Salah from the bench, off the back of another no-show in midweek in the Champions League.

Instead, 17-year-old Rio Ngumoha was afforded his first-ever Premier League start.

The teenager impressed individually but, as has happened far too frequently this season for the reigning champions, Liverpool as a team did not.

Former stalwart Jamie Carragher accused them of “sleepwalking through a game” once again and you could understand why.



Spurs had not won a Premier League game since December 28, a record run of 11 fixtures, and were devoid of confidence.

Indeed, only they and Sheffield Wednesday in the entire top four divisions have failed to win a league match in 2026 – and the Owls are on -6 points in the Championship.

Yet they looked the most competitive they have in months here at Anfield.

A ground, lest any fan forget, that they had shipped at least four in their last four trips in all competitions.

Brazilian Souza is just 19 and only arrived in January from Santos.



But he showed no fear with a ferocious blast on 12 minutes that his experienced compatriot Alisson did well to tip over the Liverpool goal.

It was just a shame for Tottenham that their own stopper Vicario could not get as strong a hand when dealing with the game’s first important moment.

Souza was the offender to bring about Szoboszlai’s free-kick, catching Alexis Mac Allister in full flow.

It was a minor contact, but a contact nonetheless.

Szoboszlai’s 25-yard strike was full of power and a bit of swerve, but fundamentally near the middle of Vicario’s goal.



Vicario was on his tiptoes and did not seem to get the leap required.

And though he got a hand to the delivery, it was not powerful enough to prevent it from crashing into the net.

The camera panned to Kinksy on the touchline afterwards and reminded everyone that, as terribly as the young Czech’s night had gone in Madrid, there was a reason why Tudor had given him the chance in the first place.

Remarkably, this was the 11th league goal Vicario had shipped from a shot outside the box this season, four worse than any other keeper.



Having been battered 5-1, 4-0, 4-2 and 4-3 in their last four trips to this ground, and given their horrendous form, a capitulation was the expectation.

But Tudor’s side did not do that and in fact had plenty of chances to get themselves back into the game.

The majority fell to Richarlison, a player who had scored four times at Anfield previously in his career.

Yet his two headers right at the end of the first half could not find the net, with the first drifting narrowly wide and the second smartly stopped by Alisson.

But it was his effort right at the end of the second half that snatched the point for Spurs.



Andy Robertson failed to deal with a long ball from Vicario, allowing sub Randal Kolo Muani to tee up Richarlison.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The Brazilian applied a scruffy finish but would not have cared a jot as it rolled into the corner.

Spurs will be desperately hoping they themselves can turn a corner now after showing they still have some fight left in them under Tudor’s rule.

Football.title: Fans convinced Tottenham manager Igor Tudor approached wrong bald man while thinking it was Arne Slot
Football.content:
FANS were convinced bumbling Igor Tudor approached the wrong bald man thinking it was Arne Slot.

The Tottenham boss has endured a nightmare start to life in the North London hotseat.





He lost his first four matches – including the 5-2 defeat at Atletico Madrid – as Spurs plunged into a serious relegation scrap.

And things appeared to go from very, very bad to somehow even worse before Sunday’s match away at Liverpool even got underway.

The TV cameras showed Tudor on the Anfield sideline before kick-off.

And he went up to a bloke standing with his arms crossed on the side of the pitch in a dark coat.

Tudor tapped him on the shoulder to say hello and shake hands before the game got underway – possibly thinking it was opposite number Slot.

But when he turned around, it was not Slot – but was in fact Tottenham’s very own player liaison officer Allan Dixon, who many reckon is a lookalike for Brad Friedel.

Dixon was standing just in front of the Tottenham dugout when Tudor tapped his side and gave him a side hug.



The Tottenham backroom staff member tapped Tudor on the front.

CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

Tudor immediately smiled and styled it out if he was mistaken.

But viewers noted that he quickly looked across towards the Liverpool bench and walked over with his hand outstretched.

However, the cameras did not pick up whether Tudor went straight over to greet Slot – although they were pictured embracing before the game.

Either way, whether Tudor mucked up or knew it was Dixon, supporters flooded social media with reaction.



One said: “Funny scenes – Igor Tudor thought he was greeting Arne Slot but it was another bald man.”

Another wrote: “AWKWARD MOMENT ALERT! Igor Tudor Tottenham Manager tried to greet Arne Slot in the Liverpool vs Spurs. Only to realise it was actually a staff member Allan Dixon, another bald man. Classic case of mistaken identity!”

A third added: “Did Igor Tudor think this was Arne Slot?”

A fourth typed: “Tudor 100 per cent thought that was Slot.”

A fifth commented: “Igor Tudor confused Allan Dixon for Arne Slot.”



And a final user replied: “Tudor genuinely thought that this was Arne Slot. Kind of sums it all up really doesn’t it.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Tottenham’s goalkeeping struggles continued in the first half when Guglielmo Vicario got a hand to Dominik Szoboszlai’s free-kick – but could only help it into the back of the net.

However, Spurs managed to claim a 1-1 draw thanks to a late leveller from Richarlison.

Football.title: ‘Tottenham have got huge problems’ – Jamie Carragher leads frustration as Vicario costs Spurs again in Liverpool clash
Football.content:
JAMIE CARRAGHER ripped into Guglielmo Vicario after the Tottenham keeper failed to keep out Dominik Szoboszlai’s first half free-kick that put Liverpool ahead.

Spurs are in serious danger of being relegated from the Premier League for the first time in their history.





And they went into the showdown at Anfield only separated from the dreaded drop zone on goal difference following Nottingham Forest’s draw with Fulham earlier in the day.

But Tottenham’s hopes of pulling off a shock result against the reigning Premier League champions suffered a huge blow when Szoboszlai broke the deadlock.

Vicario, 29, got a hand to the Hungarian midfielder’s free-kick, which was nowhere near the corner.

However he was unable to stop the ball going in.



Sky Sports co-commentator Carragher slammed Spurs’ No1 for failing to keep the strike out.

The former Liverpool defender said: “There is no doubt that Liverpool have a free-kick specialist.

“But Tottenham don’t have a goalkeeping specialist. You have to save that. Awful.”

Clinton Morrison also criticised Vicario on BBC Radio Five Live, adding: “It is a fantastic free-kick. The whip, dip and swerve of it – it is a brilliant free-kick.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

“But it wasn’t one of Dominik Szoboszlai’s best ones, and it is poor goalkeeping from Guglielmo Vicario.”

Vicario returned to the starting line-up in place of Antonin Kinsky – who was brutally hauled off after making two errors during Tottenham’s Champions League mauling at Atletico Madrid earlier this week.

And Spurs fans quickly flooded onto social media to lambast Vicario after allowing Liverpool to take the lead.



One wrote: “I will say it again, Vicario has the worst positioning I’ve ever seen.

“Abysmal, he makes Heurelho Gomes look like Petr Cech.”

Another added: “That is atrocious goalkeeping from Vicario. Justice for Kinsky.”

A third chimed in: “Anything on target beats Vicario.”

Tottenham managed to come from behind and snatch a last-gasp 1-1 draw thanks to Richarlison’s leveller.

Football.title: Chelsea and England legend Ashley Cole lands first job in management in shock move to Serie B club
Football.content:
ASHLEY COLE has landed his first job in management in a shock move.

The Chelsea and England legend takes up his maiden managerial gig in Serie B with Cesena FC.





Cole has signed a short-term deal, with the option to extend the contract if he meets “certain conditions”.

He will bring former Chelsea academy coach Jack Mesure in as his assistant.

Cesena are currently sitting eighth in Serie B, having sacked former boss Michele Mignani on Saturday afternoon.

Cole, 45, is no stranger to the coaching side of the game, after taking on various roles since hanging up his boots.

He had spells alongside Frank Lampard at Chelsea and Everton, when his old team-mate was manager, while he also coached at Derby County.

He assisted Lee Carsley during his interim spell in charge of the England national team.

In September 2024, he was appointed a full-time national coach by The FA.

The Three Lions legend, who won 107 caps for his country, also has experience working with Birmingham City.



Now he will not only make the step up to being the main man, but he will do it on foreign soil as he begins his first step on the manager ladder.

CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

That is the cut off point to enter the preliminary play-offs for a shot at promotion to Serie A and the big time.

The winners in the games between 5th and 8th advance to join those finishing third and fourth in the semi-finals.

Palermo are currently 17 points ahead of Cole’s new side in fourth meaning any hopes of going up will need to be done the hard way.

Venezia and Monza are the current top two in the division.



Cesena are on the road at Mantova on Tuesday night, but that might come too soon for Cole to be on the sidelines.

They play at home to Catanzaro next Saturday.

Football.title: Former Man City star puts call out to English players to join Marcus Rashford in La Liga
Football.content:
Former Man City star puts call out to English players to join Marcus Rashford in La Liga

Marcus Rashford has been one of several English players to take La Liga by storm in recent seasons

View 2 Images Marcus Rashford has found his groove again since joining Barcelona (Image: Getty Images)

Espanyol winger Tyrhys Dolan has called on more young footballers to make the move from England to Spain and follow Marcus Rashford's footsteps. We’ve seen more and more English footballers look to break into first-team football by joining top European leagues over the past decade than ever before.

‌



Spain’s top division, La Liga, has recently welcomed world renowned England internationals Jude Bellingham, Trent Alexander-Arnold and Rashford, who have achieved varying degrees of success there.

‌



Another lesser known Englishman who decided to move to Spain is Dolan, who left Blackburn Rovers to join Espanyol on a free transfer last summer. The 24-year-old former Manchester City academy starlet, who came through the ranks with Cole Palmer, has had an impressive debut season for the Barcelona-based side.

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Dolan made 47 appearances for Blackburn last season but playing top flight football for Espanyol this term has provided a greater challenge, one he has fully risen to as his side looks to qualify for European football this season. And he hopes others can follow the example set by himself, Bellingham and Rashford to benefit their own careers.

When asked whether he thinks other English youngsters should join La Liga, Dolan said: "Definitely. Without a doubt. I'd say if you've got the opportunity to play in the top leagues abroad, then you should go for it.

‌



Barcelona draw up Marcus Rashford back-up plan as two transfers eyed in case deal collapses READ MORE :

"I think you've seen what it’s like. It's obviously not easy. Not everyone who has come from England to play in La Liga has hit the ground running but I just think what it can do for your career is amazing.

"Just in terms of learning and adding more tools to your trade. I think, for me, I've learned so much in maybe eight or nine months. I feel like I've learnt a lot football-wise and about myself. So yeah, without a doubt, I'd definitely say it's [worth doing] if you’ve got the opportunity to take it."

View 2 Images Tyrhys Dolan signed for Espanyol last summer (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Sky Sports discounted Premier League and EFL package This article contains affiliate links, we will receive a commission on any sales we generate from it. Sky will show at least 215 live Premier League games next season, an increase of up to 100 more.

He has managed four assists and a goal in 26 La Liga appearances this season. Meanwhile, Rashford has also enjoyed a stellar run of form, managing 13 goals and ten assists across all competitions, with Barcelona expected to make his loan deal permanent this summer.

Dolan went on to explain how adapting to a new league has helped improve his own game.

‌



He said: "For me, it's little things like body positioning in terms of if players are attacking. I know I'm an attacker but that's a defensive role because we're such a hardworking team and just the positions I'm taking up on the pitch, like where to be when the ball's in a certain area, how to produce more of an end product and get more crosses in.

"There's been so many things because, when you step up a level, naturally you need to raise your game in all areas and the staff have been great working one-to-one with me and a lot of off-pitch conversations to do that.

"So yeah, there's a lot of aspects to the game and I feel like I'm understanding the game a lot more now. Hopefully I can keep doing more of that and the successes will come with it."

Football.title: Jamie Carragher eviscerates Liverpool and questions players' character after Spurs slip up
Football.content:
Jamie Carragher eviscerates Liverpool and questions players' character after Spurs slip up

Jamie Carragher didn't hold back when speaking about Liverpool's players following the 1-1 Premier League draw with Tottenham Hotspur at Anfield

View 2 Images Jamie Carragher took aim at Liverpool's players following the draw with Tottenham Hotspur

Jamie Carragher told Liverpool’s players they don’t have it in them to dig in and make it difficult for the opposition after they slipped up against Tottenham. What has fast turned into a poor campaign for the Reds took another turn for the worse against Igor Tudor’s side.

‌



Despite taking the lead through Dominik Szoboszlai’s fine free-kick after just 18 minutes, Richarlison struck a minute from time to secure his side a precious point in their battle to avoid the drop. And Carragher, speaking in the immediate aftermath of the full-time whistle going, couldn’t help but take aim at Liverpool’s players.

‌



He said: "It was an awful performance. No disrespect to Tottenham, but they are the worst team in the Premier League now, probably for the last couple of months.

‌



“They are exactly who you want to play, playing at home, the incentive of your results this weekend, and to put in a performance like that. But it's been like that all season.

"And the big question on everybody's lips is that down to the manager or the makeup of the squad? Could a new manager completely revitalise these type of players and bring that energy and sort of intensity back into this team? I'm not sure.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Richarlison stuns Liverpool with late goal as Tottenham earn lifeline - 5 talking points READ MORE :

Igor Tudor makes embarrassing error before Liverpool match as Arne Slot mix-up captured READ MORE :

"I think a lot of it goes back to what happened in the summer, the profile of the players that came in, and there's just too many players who just want to play when the ball's at their feet.

“They don't want to dig in and make it difficult. That's why it's so easy to play against this Liverpool team."

And speaking after Richarlison’s equaliser, Carragher claimed: "Well it was coming. I've been saying all second-half, Liverpool have been sleepwalking into this result.

‌



“And it was always going to be him, he's looked the most dangerous Tottenham player. And Liverpool just don't challenge for the long ball.

View 2 Images Spurs star Richarlison celebrates after scoring against Liverpool (Image: Michael Regan/Getty Images)

“Andy Robertson doesn't challenge for the long hopeful ball. Does the jump, gets in-between, not sure where Van Dijk's going - doesn't react quickly enough and then Liverpool are all over the place.

‌



“But I can't say Tottenham don't deserve it, they've had chances in the game. The majority fell to this man, but this was definitely their best one.

“Van Dijk is furious, but this has been coming...so obvious. As I said, you can't say they don't deserve it."

Football.title: Igor Tudor in tense Sky Sports interview after Liverpool draw as Spurs boss hits out at question
Football.content:
Igor Tudor in tense Sky Sports interview after Liverpool draw as Spurs boss hits out at question

Tottenham Hotspur head coach Igor Tudor engaged in a back-and-forth with Sky Sports reporter Patrick Davidson about being asked again about his future with Spurs

View 2 Images Igor Tudor's interviews continue to be appointment viewing

Igor Tudor called out Sky Sports reporter Patrick Davidson during a live interview after being asked about his Tottenham Hotspur future. The interim Spurs boss insisted that head coaches should not answer such questions and that they should instead be put to the club, as they make the decisions.

‌



Tudor spoke after Tottenham drew 1-1 with Liverpool at Anfield. Richarlison scored a late equaliser for the visitors to cancel out a free-kick from Dominik Szoboszlai that opened the scoring on Sunday.

‌



After losing his first five matches in charge, Tudor's team finally picked up a point. However, there are still questions about his future on the sideline.

‌



Igor Tudor makes embarrassing error before Liverpool match as Arne Slot mix-up captured READ MORE :

Jamie Carragher eviscerates Liverpool and questions players' character after Spurs slip up READ MORE :

Davidson asked: "Have you shown that you can provide that hope? That you don't need a new manager?"

Tudor responded by appearing to put his hand on the interviewer's shoulder before interrupting his question. The Croatian interjected: "It's enough, I always tell you what I think."

The reporter responded: "But do you not think sometimes these questions need to be asked and need to be answered? You've got a lot of Spurs fans all around the world..."

‌



Having heard enough, Tudor replied: "That's not a question for me, I'm the coach. You need to ask me about how we play." Davidson insisted: "I have," with Tudor agreeing: "Yeah, and I answered.

View 2 Images Igor Tudor has lost five of his six matches as Tottenham Hotspur head coach (Image: Getty Images)

"This question journalists like to ask is a question that doesn't have sense, when you ask the coach about their position, this doesn't make any sense. All the coaches are always telling you the same things, but you always ask this.

‌



"So the coach needs to answer something because he's obliged to come on this press conference; otherwise, he would like to stay home, but you always insist with almost the same questions."

Davidson explained: "No, but the point being, you can provide the hope, right? That's something you said in an interview on Friday," before being cut off again.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Tudor claimed: "I didn't say that. What did I say?" The reporter reiterated: "You said people need hope of a new manager sometimes, and my point is only, have you shown today with the way Tottenham play that you can provide hope?"

The Spurs boss asked in response: "What do you think I will answer now? What I will answer, do you probably hope? What kind of questions? What do I need to tell you now? No?"

Davidson clarified: "You can say whatever you like." Tudor continued: "I tell you this, what will I tell my players? Of course, so this is the answer you want to hear."

Article continues below

The reporter then repeated: "You can just say whatever you like." Tudor then looked away, before asking: "Did we finish, or no?" To which Davidson agreed: "We're finished."

Football.title: Arne Slot's reaction to Richarlison goal speaks volumes as Liverpool failings resurface
Football.content:
Arne Slot's reaction to Richarlison goal speaks volumes as Liverpool failings resurface

LIVERPOOL 1-1 TOTTENHAM: Arne Slot's side sleepwalked into a 1-1 draw with relegation-battling Tottenham, missing the chance to go fourth in the Premier League table

View 2 Images Arne Slot and Igor Tudor felt contrasting emotions (Image: Carl Recine/Getty Images)

One has a single point from his four Premier League matches, one won the Premier League title in his first season. But Arne Slot looked like the manager under pressure when Richarlison mis-hit a scruffy equaliser as the Anfield clock ticked towards added time.

‌



Igor Tudor, whose calamitous spell as Spurs manager could still end soon, leapt into and punched the air, while Slot could only shake his head in disbelief. This result was a significant blow for Slot and Liverpool but a bigger issue for the Dutchman is the growing discontent around Anfield.

‌



The nature of the setback - Liverpool yet again conceding a late goal - was certain to prompt some jeering but there was not a great amount of enthusiasm for the home performance ahead of Richarlison's leveller. In fact, Liverpool had only been ahead through a goalkeeping error.

‌



Dominik Szoboszlai has established himself as one of the Premier League's most lethal set-piece exponents and his first half strike was typically sweet. It was firmly hit and had a smidgeon of sideways movement but Guglielmo Vicario really should have saved it. In fact, he probably should have shuffled across and caught it.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Richarlison stuns Liverpool with late goal as Tottenham earn lifeline - 5 talking points READ MORE :

Igor Tudor makes embarrassing error before Liverpool match as Arne Slot mix-up captured READ MORE :

Perhaps Tudor has managed to undermine the confidence of two 'keepers, having made the ill-fated decision not to start Vicario against Atletico Madrid. Under Tudor, Spurs have maintained their knack of conceding soft goals. In fact, they might just have upped it.

‌



Not that Szoboszlai should have held back on his celebrations. This was his fourth goal from direct free-kicks this season and his satisfaction was probably enhanced by a spot of frustration at again being deployed at right-back. Of course he performs his designated duty with diligence - and no little excellence - but it is obvious he does not relish the role.

He was there because Slot was using Jeremie Frimpong in the Mo Salah position, with Rio Ngumoha on the left flank. Such has been Salah’s extended dip in form, Slot’s decision was no headline-grabber, although you suspect the Egyptian will return for Champions League duty in midweek.

Ngumoha's contribution was exciting, if a little inconsistent. He is a raw talent, that is for sure. And the 17-year-old stood out in a Liverpool team that is still groping for its best form.

‌



Will Liverpool qualify for the Champions League? Give us your prediction in the comments section.

View 2 Images Richarlison snatched a draw in the 90th minute (Image: Darren Staples/AFP via Getty Images)

Not everything he did came off. Occasionally, he chose the wrong option. He was slightly off-cue when trying to convert a couple of half-chances. But Anfield still sang his name, still acclaimed a 17-year-old who will shine on this stage for many years to come.

‌



Ngumoha did not play in the Egyptian's position but he started and Salah did not. It felt like a significant moment in Liverpool time. Salah was sent on for half an hour and looked a certainty to score on the counter-attack on more than one occasion.

But in a microcosm of his season, his finishing was panicky. He has gone from instinctive to indecisive. Ultimately, Salah's failure to put the outcome beyond doubt proved costly as Andy Robertson failed to make a relatively simple clearance and Randal Kolo Muani set up Richarlison's equaliser.

This season, Liverpool have now conceded eight Premier League goals in the 90th minute or later and they have dropped seven points from winning positions at Anfield. No wonder the locals jeered.

Football.title: Wayne Rooney tells Man Utd to immediately end manager hunt in very clear statement
Football.content:
Wayne Rooney tells Man Utd to immediately end manager hunt in very clear statement

Wayne Rooney knows who he wants to become Manchester United's next manager and has sent a clear message to Sir Jim Ratcliffe

View 2 Images Former Manchester United forward Wayne Rooney has delivered his verdict on Michael Carrick's future (Image: Getty Images)

Wayne Rooney has claimed that Michael Carrick should "100 per cent" be given the permanent Manchester United head coach position. Carrick has done an incredible job since taking over on an interim basis and now has Rooney's full backing despite some big-name managers being linked with the post.

‌



Rooney, 40, made his position crystal clear on Carrick after United's 3-1 win over Aston Villa on Sunday. The Reds were once again good value for the three points at Old Trafford, with Casemiro, Matheus Cunha and Benjamin Sesko all scoring to tighten their grip on a top-four place.

‌



It's a result that has boosted United's prospects of achieving a Champions League comeback, with Chelsea and Liverpool also slipping up over the weekend. The Red Devils are currently third, five points above fifth-place Liverpool and six clear of Chelsea in sixth. As England are set to receive an extra spot in Europe's elite club tournament for next season, United require a maximum of 18 points from their remaining eight fixtures to guarantee a top-five finish.

‌



Man Utd chiefs issued clear transfer demand during crucial Aston Villa win READ MORE :

Should Carrick clinch the deal and deliver United's Champions League return, he would hand himself a solid opportunity of securing the role on a permanent basis in the summer, despite links to Oliver Glasner, Roberto de Zerbi, Julian Nagelsmann, Andoni Iraola and Luis Enrique.

And Rooney is no doubt about who should get the job. Speaking following Sunday's win over Villa, Rooney was questioned about whether Carrick ought to be awarded the head coach position full-time this summer, to which the ex-United striker responded: "100 per cent he should."

He added: "I knew this was gonna happen with Michael Carrick, I know him very well, I know his character and personality," Rooney added. "It needed a calm head, but someone who knows the place and the players needed some love and he's give them that. We're seeing the players play with more quality, we're seeing them more together as a team and they look like a very strong team.

‌



"So, for me, why would you change? You've got someone come in who's got I think the best win percentage of any Manchester United manager after that many games, so, for me, he has to get the job."

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Carrick rejoined the Reds in mid-January on an interim arrangement until the end of the campaign and has enhanced the club's performances since his appointment. Indeed, the 44-year-old has claimed seven victories, drawn one and suffered one defeat from his nine fixtures after becoming the interim head coach and replacing Darren Fletcher.

‌



Incorporating three fixtures he oversaw in interim charge during 2021, Carrick boasts a victory rate exceeding 70 per cent throughout his spell in the United dugout and is quickly establishing himself as the top candidate.

Even Roy Keane, who was initially against Carrick getting the job permanently, and predicted earlier in the season that United were 'not ready' to crack the top four, has changed his tune.

View 2 Images Michael Carrick's Manchester United beat Aston Villa 3-1 (Image: PA)

‌



The ex-United midfielder is now convinced his former side will be playing in Europe's elite competition next season. He told Sky Sports on Sunday: "Yeah, I think certs for it. No distractions, just focus on the games, get some recovery.

"Villa looked a bit flat today [after playing in Europa League on Thursday], United in the second half were pretty comfortable.

"If you give players like Bruno [Fernandes] time and space, you're going to be punished. I think United are absolute certs for that Champions League spot."

Article continues below

Carrick and United are next in action when they make the trip to Bournemouth on Friday. A victory for the Reds would secure them at minimum a nine-point cushion above sixth position going into the March international break.

Football.title: Michael Carrick answers question on possible Casemiro U-turn after eye-catching display
Football.content:
Michael Carrick answers question on possible Casemiro U-turn after eye-catching display

Manchester United fans made their feelings on Casemiro clear during the Red Devils' 3-1 win over Aston Villa on Sunday, as he enters the final eight matches of his tenure at Old Trafford

View 2 Images Michael Carrick has spoken about Casemiro after his goal for Manchester United against Aston Villa (Image: Catherine Ivill - AMA, Getty Images)

Michael Carrick still believes that Casemiro will leave Manchester United this summer, despite suggestions that the club could try to convince him to stay. Both parties have agreed that the 34-year-old will leave the club when his contract expires at the end of this season.

‌



However, his recent performances have been outstanding, as evidenced by him playing a part in all three goals as United thumped Aston Villa 3-1 on Sunday. Casemiro netted the first goal and then provided crucial passes leading up to goals for Matheus Cunha and Benjamin Sesko, bolstering United's hold on a Champions League spot for 2026/27.

‌



The ex-Real Madrid midfielder has featured in all nine matches under Carrick's management so far and is showcasing some of the finest football of his four-year tenure at Old Trafford. However, he earns £350,000-a-week, and his departure this summer will aid in trimming United's wage bill.

‌



Man Utd chiefs issued clear transfer demand during crucial Aston Villa win READ MORE :

But during Sunday's victory, the Stretford End chanted 'one more year, one more year Casemiro', and Carrick was questioned whether a reversal could be made before the season's end. He replied: "I think in some ways it's difficult to say, I think it's when something's decided and in some ways the fact that it was decided makes things a little bit easier and everyone understands the situation really.

"I think the impact he's had has been terrific, certainly since I've been here and working with him and his influence within the team and big moments and goals. It was a nice moment at the end there with the supporters and having that connection, and the respect."

United could save upwards of £18million annually in wages when Casemiro leaves, but finding a replacement will be an enormous challenge. He's netted seven goals in the Premier League this campaign and has rediscovered his form to become one of the division's outstanding holding midfielders.

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

As a five-time Champions League victor with Real Madrid, he also brings considerable experience to the club, though Carrick dismissed suggestions that replacing him would be a near-impossible undertaking. He added: "I think, this is totally no disrespect to Case, he's been fantastic, he has been a big player for us and has been important in the dressing room and one that I have spoken to and connected with really well.

"But I think as a club and as a team, your players come and go, some may be bigger, some may be more important than others at different times.

‌



View 2 Images Casemiro will leave Man United this summer (Image: OLI SCARFF, AFP via Getty Images)

"I don't think it's ever really about replacing them like for like, I think you can go with different directions, you understand what the balance of the squad will need, whether that's on the pitch, off the pitch leadership, positionally, there's all sorts of different things going into it.

"Case's done some really, really good things and certainly since I've been here he's been an absolute pleasure to work with."

Football.title: Richarlison stuns Liverpool with late goal as Tottenham earn lifeline - 5 talking points
Football.content:
Richarlison stuns Liverpool with late goal as Tottenham earn lifeline - 5 talking points

LIVERPOOL 1-1 TOTTENHAM: Ex-Everton star Richarlison was the hero for Spurs, handing Igor Tudor a lifeline and giving his team a much-needed boost in their fight for Premier League survival

View 5 Images Richarlison scored a 90th-minute equaliser (Image: Michael Regan/Getty Images)

Pantomime villain Richarlison rescued a precious point for Tottenham after Liverpool sleepwalked into a 1-1 draw. The former Everton star netted a goal in the 90th minute to silence the Kop End and boost Spurs' survival hopes.

‌



It'd looked like the end for Tottenham boss Igor Tudor, who was on the cusp of his fifth defeat in as many games. Dominik Szoboszlai had given Liverpool an early lead and the Reds were on course to jump into fourth place.

‌



Manchester United had done Arne Slot's side a favour by beating Aston Villa 3-1 earlier on Sunday, but the reigning champions couldn't take advantage. Instead, Spurs managed to match fellow strugglers West Ham and Nottingham Forest's results with a draw of their own.

‌



Here are five talking points from Anfield.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Igor Tudor appears to make embarrassing Arne Slot gaffe ahead of Liverpool clash READ MORE :

Liverpool activate Alisson contract option as former boss eyes reunion READ MORE :

1. Slot rings the changes

Trailing in their Champions League last-16 tie with Galatasaray, some rotation was on the cards for Liverpool. But few at Anfield expected both Hugo Ekitike and Mo Salah to come out of the starting XI.

‌



Rio Ngumoha was handed his first Premier League start on the left wing while Cody Gakpo played as a striker. Jamie Carragher reacted to the team news by saying: "A little bit of a shock because Arne Slot normally likes to keep a settled team, and he's done that for the last few weeks, especially after Mo Salah got back from AFCON.

"There's a big game in midweek, so he [Slot] obviously sees this as a game that he can afford to make changes in."

Will Tottenham get relegated? Give us your prediction in the comments section.

‌



View 5 Images Rio Ngumoha was handed his first Premier League start (Image: Liverpool FC/Liverpool FC via Getty Images)

2. Szoboszlai makes Liverpool history

Some things don't change. Like Szoboszlai scoring free-kicks and Tottenham having a poor goalkeeper.

When Archie Gray fouled Alexis Mac Allister around 20 yards from goal, many in the Kop End would've put money on the ball hitting the back of the net. Indeed it did, although Szboszlai didn't need to produce one of his best to break the deadlock - more on that later.

‌



The Reds' makeshift right-back has now scored four direct free-kicks in the Premier League this season - the most ever by a Liverpool player in a single campaign in the competition.

View 5 Images Dominik Szoboszlai is a certified free-kick specialist (Image: Liverpool FC/Liverpool FC via Getty Images)

3. Vicario gives Tottenham deja vu

Back in goal after his 17-minute spell on the bench in midweek, Guglielmo Vicario had a point to prove. On his first assignment, the Italian failed.

‌



Szoboszlai's free-kick was palmed in by Vicario, and Tottenham's defenders must've had a serious case of deja vu after Antonin Kinsky's nightmare against Atletico Madrid. Whether it be poor footwork or weak hands, Vicario was fault and gave Spurs an even bigger challenge.

View 5 Images Guglielmo Vicario's form has plummeted this season (Image: Robbie Jay Barratt - AMA/Getty Images)

4. Richarlison rages after Van Dijk battle

A familiar foe in these parts, Richarlison was always going to find himself in a contentious situation. The Brazilian scored the all-important equaliser but was involved in an incident beforehand.

‌



After Dominic Solanke brushed off Joe Gomez, Richarlison got himself in a foot race with Virgil van Dijk. The Liverpool captain was grappling with the Tottenham forward, who went bundling over in the penalty area.

Richarlison was adamant that Spurs should've been awarded a penalty, claiming that he'd had his shirt pulled, but referee Chris Kavanagh wasn't interested. Nor was VAR, as Spurs continued to toil in their pursuit of an equaliser.

View 5 Images Richarlison believed that Tottenham should've had a penalty (Image: Darren Staples/AFP via Getty Images)

‌



5. Who else but him?

Proudly playing his part as Anfield's pantomime villain, of course Richarlison would have another say. With Liverpool failing to put the game to bed and leaving the back door open, Randal Kolo Muani held off Van Dijk inside the penalty area to give Richarlison a simple finish.

The Tottenham hero was almost left red-faced when Liverpool broke away in stoppage time, only for Ekitike to miss a golden opportunity. Boos rang around Anfield on the full-time whistle.

Pure drama.

Football.title: Bryan Mbeumo 'shakes head' at Man Utd team-mate as Gary Neville raises concern
Football.content:
Bryan Mbeumo 'shakes head' at Man Utd team-mate as Gary Neville raises concern

Bryan Mbeumo went off during Manchester United's 3-1 win over Aston Villa, with the forward seen shaking his head at team-mate Diogo Dalot when leaving the pitch

View 2 Images Bryan Mbeumo sat down on the Old Trafford pitch before being substituted (Image: OLI SCARFF, AFP via Getty Images)

Gary Neville expressed concerns over a potential injury to Bryan Mbeumo after seeing him 'shaking his head' and speaking to Diogo Dalot following his substitution during Manchester United's 3-1 victory over Aston Villa at Old Trafford. Benjamin Sesko was brought on as a replacement for the 26-year-old and netted United's third goal of the match as they continued their fine form.

‌



Mbeumo has gone off in each of United's previous three games. However, he was hobbling on this occasion while walking off the pitch.

‌



The forward also appeared to exchange words with Dalot as he exited the pitch. Neville picked up on this interaction with his team-mate whilst working as a co-commentator for the match on Sky Sports.

‌



Man Utd have plan to sell five stars as brutal £422m transfer truth laid bare READ MORE :

Neville said: "Is he injured? Is he limping, Mbeumo? He was shaking his head when he walked off and looked at one of the United players as if he might be carrying something."

Mbeumo's body language will no doubt be a bit of a worry for United and Carrick. The forward now hasn't scored for United in his last five Premier League matches, but has arguably been their standout performer this season.

United were leading 2-1 at that point, with Mbeumo replaced five minutes after Matheus Cunha had restored their advantage. Casemiro had given the hosts an early second-half lead before Ross Barkley levelled for Villa in controversial circumstances, with Amadou Onana narrowly avoiding a VAR decision against him.

‌



Sesko then added United's third and final goal within five minutes of coming on for Mbeumo. They maintained control of the game from that point, with Manuel Ugarte replacing Casemiro for the final stages.

View 2 Images Gary Neville spotted Bryan Mbeumo shaking his head (Image: Lewis Storey, Getty Images)

Mbeumo has featured in every fixture under Michael Carrick, with the opening match he oversaw coinciding with the Cameroon international's return from the Africa Cup of Nations. Nevertheless, he went off in all but one of those encounters.

‌



Mbeumo eventually completed the full 90 minutes away to West Ham United, setting up Sesko's 96th-minute leveller. The duo also linked up once more for the solitary strike in United's 1-0 victory over Everton before he limped off seven minutes afterwards, being replaced by Noussair Mazraoui.

It prompted uncertainty about his availability against Crystal Palace, but he started and played 85 minutes before Zirkzee entered the fray again. Carrick also made the identical substitution in their 2-1 loss to Newcastle United.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Their next fixture is on Friday evening, with a shorter-than-usual gap before next week's journey to Bournemouth. Mbeumo appears to be an early concern for that one.

However, that represents their final match before the international break, meaning United do not play again until 13 April. Consequently, if Mbeumo does miss out on the south coast, there is a fortnight for him to recuperate before Carrick has to rely on him again.

Sunday's triumph, meanwhile, saw United strengthen their hold on a Champions League spot. Carrick's side now sit comfortably in third place and crucially, three points above fifth position.

Article continues below

Football.title: Richarlison stuns Anfield to earn precious point for Spurs
Football.content:
Richarlison scored a 90th-minute equaliser as Tottenham stunned Anfield to draw 1-1 with Liverpool as Igor Tudor finally stopped the rot in dramatic fashion.

When Guglielmo Vicario, the goalkeeper recalled after Antonin Kinsky's nightmare in Spain, contrived to let in Dominik Szoboszlai's first-half free-kick, Spurs were facing a seventh straight defeat in all competitions - and a fifth since Tudor took over.

But Arne Slot's side failed to press home their advantage in the second half, Richarlison having four chances before he finally did level it in the final minute of the 90. The result means Spurs are one point above the drop zone. Liverpool are fifth, below Aston Villa.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Spurs forward Richarlison fires home equaliser in 90th-minute against Liverpool

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Dominik Szoboszlai puts Liverpool ahead as Jamie Carragher has harsh words for Guglielmo Vicario

Player ratings: Liverpool: Alisson (5), Szoboszlai (7), Gomez (6), Van Dijk (6), Robertson (5), Gravenberch (6), Mac Allister (5), Frimpong (7), Ngumoha (8), Wirtz (7), Gakpo (7).



Subs: Salah (7), Ekitike (6), Jones (5), Nyoni (6), Chiesa (n/a).



Tottenham: Vicario (6), Porro (7), Danso (6), Dragusin (5); Spence (7), Sarr (7), Gray (7), Souza (6), Richarlison (8), Solanke (6), Tel (7).



Subs: Simons (6), Kolo Muani (7), Olusesi (6), Rowswell (n/a).



Player of the Match: Richarlison.

Tudor's team show some fight

There was an expectation that Tudor's time was up when Tottenham capitulated against Atletico Madrid in midweek but this was a spirited showing from a depleted side that few had fancied could possibly come up with anything at Anfield. Spurs showed fight.

Without over a dozen first-team players, Tottenham had seen relegation rivals West Ham, Leeds and Nottingham Forest all pick up draws to leave them outside of the relegation zone only by goal difference at kick-off. The pressure was on but they battled.

The home game against Forest next week remains massive for the prospects of retaining their Premier League status but for the first time in a long time there is a little evidence that Spurs are prepared to scrap to stay in this. Supporters will take that right now.

Team news headlines Rio Ngumoha came into the Liverpool team for his first Premier League start. Alisson Becker was among four others to return as Mo Salah and Hugo Ekitike were benched.



Radu Dragusin and Souza came in with Cristian Romero and Micky van de Ven unavailable. Igor Tudor named only seven Tottenham substitutes.

Tudor: Team spirit was amazing

Tottenham head coach Igor Tudor speaking to Sky Sports:

"We showed the spirit, the team spirit.

"This moment is really strange but I said to the players it is even more challenging to show who we are as players, as a team. At this moment, it is really difficult to work on any kind of structure in attack, so we had to simplify it.

"The team spirit was amazing, they were very honest to give everything they had in this particular moment. This was the demand. And then the quality is there.

"Every point is crucial, every point counts. We go game by game."

What went wrong for Liverpool?

Szoboszlai's fourth free-kick goal of the Premier League season, a Liverpool record, had put Slot's side in position to bury an opponent low on confidence. Rio Ngumoha was certainly in the mood on his first Premier League start, but Liverpool did not finish it.

Richarlison had warned them, scoring with his fifth shot of the game - more than any other player. Randal Kolo Muani outmuscled Andy Robertson and Virgil van Dijk to set up the chance, another damning reflection of Liverpool's defending.

They allowed the opposition back in and paid the price, a feature of their title defence. Liverpool had lost their previous game in the competition against bottom club Wolves but this was in front of their own fans, who booed at the full-time whistle.

Awful by Liverpool, says Carragher

Sky Sports' Jamie Carragher at Anfield:

"It was an awful performance. No disrespect to Tottenham but they are the worst team in the Premier League now, probably for the last couple of months. They are exactly who you want to play, playing at home, the incentive of your results this weekend and to put in performance like that. But it's been like that all season.

"And the big question on everybody's lips - is that down to the manager or the makeup of the squad? Could a new manager completely revitalise these type of players and bring that energy and sort of intensity back into this team? I'm not sure.

"I think a lot of it goes back to what happened in the summer, the profile of the players that came in, and there's just too many players who just want to play when the ball's at their feet. They don't want to dig in and make it difficult. That's why it's so easy to play against this Liverpool team."

Story of the match in stats

Football.title: Carragher slams Liverpool display vs Spurs as hopes of CL spot hit
Football.content:
Jamie Carragher has branded Liverpool's 1-1 draw with Tottenham as a "disaster" and castigated the performance of Arne Slot's misfiring side.

Richarlison's late leveller grabbed a vital point for Tottenham in their fight against relegation but dealt a heavy blow to Liverpool's Champions League qualification hopes.

Liverpool are fifth, a point above Chelsea and two behind Aston Villa.

"This is an absolute disaster for Liverpool in terms of their Champions League qualification," said Carragher.

"They have actually made Tottenham look like a decent team."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Spurs forward Richarlison fires home equaliser in 90th-minute against Liverpool.

Ex-Everton striker Richarlison had squandered a number of chances before finding the net in front of the Kop in the 90th minute.

Liverpool hit by another stoppage-time goal Liverpool have now conceded eight Premier League goals in the 90th minute or later this season - their most in a single campaign.

"The defending was absolutely shocking from Liverpool. I've been saying all half that Liverpool have been sleepwalking into this.

Liverpool drop crucial points again Liverpool have dropped seven points from winning positions at Anfield in the Premier League this season, their most since the 2016-17 campaign (also 7).

"It was an awful performance. No disrespect to Tottenham but they are the worst team in the Premier League now, probably for the last couple of months. They are exactly who you want to play, playing at home, the incentive of your results this weekend and to put in a performance like that. But it's been like that all season.

"And the big question on everybody's lips is that down to the manager or the makeup of the squad? Could a new manager completely revitalise these type of players and bring that energy and sort of intensity back into this team? I'm not sure.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Dominik Szoboszlai puts Liverpool ahead from a free-kick but Jamie Carragher had some harsh words for the Spurs goalkeeper Guglielmo Vicario.

"I think a lot of it goes back to what happened in the summer, the profile of the players that came in and there's just too many players who just want to play when the ball's at their feet. They don't want to dig in and make it difficult. That's why it's so easy to play against this Liverpool team."

Szoboszlai: We must wake up or settle for Conference League

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Speaking after the 1-1 draw with Tottenham in the Premier League, Dominik Szoboszlai suggested that Liverpool's performance levels need to improve otherwise they'll be forced to settle for Conference League football next season.

Dominik Szoboszlai says Liverpool should be happy to qualify for the Conference League if they continue to play like they did in their draw with Tottenham.

"We have to wake up. If we keep going like this, next season we should be happy if we play Conference League," Szoboszlai told Sky Sports.

"In the first half, we played very well. We controlled the whole game, they hardly created many chances.

"Second half, I don't know, we just don't do the same things like in the first half. The question is why? We will sit down together. This is the most difficult time. We have to stick together.

"I didn't hear the boos [at full-time] but I can understand because last season we became champions and this season we are not performing in the way that we should be.

"They should stay behind us because last season, when we were champions four games before the end, everybody was happy. Support us now when we're in difficult times."

Slot on familiar failings costing them vs Spurs

Liverpool head coach Arne Slot aired his frustration at the familiar way his side dropped points against Spurs.

He was keen to flag some positive aspects to his team's performance but ultimately rued their failure to score more of their chances on Sunday.

"Every time when it happens it feels like it's the biggest blow. When it happened against Wolves, it felt like it was the biggest blow of the season and now it happens again.

"Again, in the last minute, we get an opportunity to score afterwards ourselves, which we didn't do, and then we leave the pitch with a disappointing result.

"The way we concede is not always the same in the last minute, so you can't compare the deflected shot against Wolves with the one we conceded today, or the 30-yard shot against Fulham in the top corner, so it's different in the way we concede, but the same thing is that we constantly - or mainly - if we drop points, concede goals either in extra time or as today in the last minute.

"It's clear that there were a few things positive today.

"If you look at the great goal we scored with Dominik, if you look at the performance of Rio [Ngumoha] for a 17-year-old, if you look at the chances we've created... But we are never able to outscore the amount of chances we have, so you always expect more goals than we are creating.

"But it's also completely clear that there are parts of our game where we definitely need to improve, and that's also why we are in this position, because not everything, actually, a lot of things are not perfect. And as a result of that, we're not able to win or to get as many points as we want to."

To put Slot's comment about chance conversion in context, in the Premier League this season Liverpool have scored 49 times from a total Expected Goals (xG) figure of 50.01.

So Liverpool have performed almost exactly to their xG. Ten teams have a higher underperformance.

In fact, across the last eight Premier League games, Liverpool have scored 16 goals from an xG of just 14.3.

Against Spurs, they had an xG of 1.67 compared to the one goal they did score.

Football.title: Thrilling Six Nations leaves questions for England, Scotland, Ireland, Wales
Football.content:
It is being touted as the most entertaining Six Nations ever, and looking at the pre-tournament script compared to the end credits, you can see just why.

'Unpredictability' was the defining characteristic of a Six Nations showing that left the audience gripped at every twist and turn.

It was a non-stop action blockbuster with a finale that lived up to the hype as a total of 29 tries were scored across 'Super Saturday', the most ever for a single day in the championship's history and two more than the previous highest in 2015.

Image: France are Six Nations champions once again

Six teams, six potential protagonists gave their all, and while they now lick their wounds and prepare in anticipation of another battle in the near future, the dissection gets under way of just how and why France were the heroes of the tale once again.

It is a series that begs the questions of why England could not live up to their hype, why Scotland can be in the race but never win it, if Italy have the potential for 'leading man' status, if Wales can be restored to their former glory, and if Ireland's time in the spotlight is coming to a close.

A walking contradiction: France's attacking nous the key

Image: They get to celebrate retaining the title for the first time since 2007

Talk about delivering drama! France did that with the flair and freedom we have come to expect on a Saturday night in Paris.

Indeed, their attacking ability truly has to be commended; it is what got them over the line and has helped them to successive titles for the first time since 2006 and 2007.

They can create a try from nowhere, they are littered with stars that can do the unthinkable, they say: 'you score 30 points, we will score 31'.

Twitter Twitter , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Twitter cookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the This content is provided by, which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enablecookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the Privacy Options Unfortunately we have been unable to verify if you have consented to Twitter cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Twitter cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

Les Blues started with a rout of Ireland in the French capital and ended by breaking Irish hearts with a thriller against England in the same spot. They started with composure, it ended with chaos but that will not matter- what will matter is that they have secured their eighth title.

That being said, while no one has scored more tries than France in this Six Nations, no one has conceded more tries than them either. They scored 30 but they conceded 19.

In the last two matches alone, they conceded 96 points and 14 tries. Yet, they still took the win.

Image: Thomas Ramos came up with the clutch penalty conversion that secured the thrilling win

In this current moment, I imagine sipping champagne is more the course of action than analysis of their defensive fragility but once the Parisian party stops and the work starts once again, they will have to work out whether their defensive limits are a by-product of modern rugby union or something that can be majorly fixed.

Their own defensive masterminds do not seem too worried.

"Rugby at the moment, particularly the Six Nations, is just phenomenal," said France defence coach Shaun Edwards.

"I remember coaching a team in Wales who went five games without conceding one try and that's impossible now. It is for the better of the game."

Signs of life: England exit on optimistic defeat

Image: England captain Maro Itoje has said he things his side are 'going places' despite their dismal campaign

England come away from the Six Nations with their worst campaign ever, picking up just one win and four losses. Despite that worrying statistic, their performance against France in an epic contest showed not just signs of life, but that they could be regalvanised.

After the buoyancy of a convincing opening-round win over Wales, England fans watched their side's ill discipline and costly errors lead to losses against Scotland, Ireland, and for the first time ever, Italy.

The pressure was high and the eyeballs were ever-present as Steve Borthwick's side tried to convince onlookers that there were no rifts, that they could win, that green shoots of hope were on the horizon.

Image: England had a stark wake up call with their loss to Italy

To be fair to them, they delivered on that despite making it four losses in a row. Throughout the contest with France, England were overall the more efficient and stronger side, dominating the France pack and creating opportunities off the back of it.

Ultimately, France were able to, as is commonplace with them, create something out of nothing and did that over and over before Thomas Ramos sent over the clutch penalty that wrapped up the enthralling 48-46 win.

The positivity for England is not to say they did not make errors - they want to ask questions about Ellis Genge's yellow card, Ollie Chessum may have ran in for his try under the sticks if he had his time again, Jack van Poortvliet may not have opted for the box kick with two minutes remaining, Henry Pollock may have kept hold of the ball rather than throwing a speculative pass with 30 seconds left on the clock.

Image: Ollie Chessum scored a stunning interception try but finished it in the corner

That being said, they have remained steadfast in their belief during this tournament that they know how to score; they just need to start turning opportunities into points. That conversion occurred as they ended with the stunning showing they provided with France.

Steve Borthwick believes he is the "right man" to keep leading England and with 46 points on 'Super Saturday', he looks to have bought himself some time to turn stunning matches into stunning wins.

Ireland take Triple Crown but left ruing opening debacle

Image: Ireland secured the Triple Crown for the fourth time in the last five years

We have been used to the Six Nations as of late being somewhat of a two-horse race between France and Ireland - the last five have gone to one of the two. That did not seem like it was going to occur once again on opening night when France came away with a 36-14 win over Ireland at the Stade de France.

You only needed to look at Andy Farrell's steely post-match demeanour to know that Ireland had been abject and he said that the loss "had to stand for something".

Five weeks later, they have a Triple Crown. They were a Thomas Ramos conversion away from being crowned champions. It very nearly did stand for everything.

Some will argue that if you need another side to do you a favour to win you a tournament, then it was not yours to win anyway. Some will argue that Ireland were nowhere near as convincing as they have been over the last few years. Some will argue Ireland grew into the tournament and that was evidenced by the fact the clinical nature of their showings got more intense as the competition went on.

Image: Andy Farrell and skipper Caelan Doris were proud of their side's win over Scotland

"I think we probably showed good resolve in bouncing back," said Ireland skipper Caelan Doris.

"The Italy performance (a 20-13 win in round two) wasn't great, but we still got the win here at home and it's been fairly linear in terms of trajectory since then.

"It's a great group, there's depth building there, there's competition building within the squad.

"We're moving things in the right way and it's been enjoyable chasing down the potential and seeing some of the growth over the last number of weeks."

For Ireland, it is now time to dissect whether they were a victim of the volatile nature of the Six Nations in 2026 or if they need to prepare, in what seems to be a period of transition, for more horses to enter the race for the title in the future.

Scotland chained to 'nearly men' tag

Image: Scotland had big results against England and France

The eternal Scottish tale unfolds once again. There was history for the taking, but they just could not do the history-making.

Instead of the aforementioned two-horse race between France and Ireland, Scotland made sure to be the third as they headed to Dublin hunting a Triple Crown.

So much history was on the line: They could have had a first Triple Crown since 1990. They could have exorcised their Dublin demons and got a first victory there since 2010. They could have stopped Ireland's double-figure run of victories against them that spans nine years.

Image: Finn Russell tried to influence the game against Ireland but they once again fell short

Instead, they left Dublin with a 43-21 loss and as the competition's eternal nearly men despite their electrifying 50-40 win over France that blew the tournament wide open.

They were the outsiders in the race, of course, but what could have been a generation‑defining triumph for Scottish rugby and for Gregor Townsend's tenure is another 'what if' and moment of torment as questions will once again swirl about the long-standing head coach leading his side into the 2027 World Cup.

Wooden spooners no more: Italy the contenders

Image: Italy pulled off their first ever Six Nations win over England

In the modern Six Nations, four different countries have walked away with the wooden spoon after finishing bottom of the table.

France have suffered that fate just once, Wales three times, and Scotland four times.

For Italy? That has happened 18 times. 18 times they have had to walk away knowing they performed the worst out of everyone, justifying their position in the prestigious tournament.

But no more do the Italian outfit perennially prop up the table. No, they are now contenders, they can now beat the big boys.

Image: Italy could not back up the win over England as they then lost to Wales

That is not to say they don't still have work to do. There is no point pulling off a history-making 23-18 win over England to then get thumped by Wales just a week later and lose the chance for your best-ever Six Nations finish.

What they need to fix up is going from a monumental win like that over England and backing it up the following match.

If they do that, there is a near future in which Italy are battling it out on 'Super Saturday'.

Three years of pain at the Principality fades away

Image: Wales picked up their first Six Nations win since 2023

1,099 days. 15 consecutive defeats. Finally, the Principality got to celebrate once again.

Yes, Wales leave the 2026 Six Nations rooted to the foot of the table, finishing last due to clinching fewer bonus points than England.

However, Cardiff got a big 'cwtch' in the form of a 31-17 win over Italy to end their tournament and finally turn that talk of progress into points.

The Welsh fans have gone through all five stages of grief since 2023. They denied the situation was as bad as it seemed, got angry that it was as bad as it seemed, bargained and brought back Warren Gatland in the hopes that would somehow rectify things, entered the 'depression' stage as things got worse than they ever thought they possibly could, before finally accepting that their side were on a journey.

Image: Wales finish with the wooden spoon but with a win that marks a turning point

Following the acceptance, they have worked hard and after the constant talk that they were focused on improving the way they played, they started to talk about winning again.

That relief arrived on March 14 2026 with Wales rightfully emotional that an enduring period of poor rugby might be coming to a close and that the fruits of their labour might be ripe once again.

"We hope we have restored some faith in the jersey and into what this group can do," said captain Dewi Lake.

"I am overjoyed for the boys because of how hard they have worked. That performance sums up how we have grown in the last two to three weeks.

"That was a performance we have built towards and this group deserves it massively.

"We have gone through a lot of emotionally tough things recently, whether that is on the field or off it."

Football.title: Neville: Man Utd right to let 'exceptional' Casemiro leave
Football.content:
Gary Neville has claimed Manchester United are still right to let Casemiro go at the end of the season despite the midfielder re-establishing himself as one of Michael Carrick's most important players in the push for Champions League football.

The Brazilian was on the scoresheet for the second consecutive game as he opened the scoring in United's 3-1 victory over Aston Villa - a result which sees them pull three points clear of Unai Emery's side in the battle for third.

Despite being rumoured to leave the club in each of the last two summers, Casemiro has remained at Old Trafford and started in each of the last 13 Premier League games, integral to United's upturn under Carrick. His seven goals are a season's best return.

In January, it was announced that the 34-year-old would leave United when his contract expires, however recent form has led fan calls for his continuation beyond the summer. Despite that, and his positive influence on the team, Neville still believes the right decision would be to allow Casemiro to leave.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Casemiro heads Manchester United in front in their Premier League clash with Aston Villa.

"Obviously, Manchester United got him at the latter part of his career. What he was is an exceptional central midfield player that played in one of the most dominant midfields that you'll ever see at Real Madrid," he said on The Gary Neville Podcast.

"But it's right that United do let him go, and it's right that he does leave United. This is like a cameo at the end of his career. Almost like a swansong where he's probably loving every minute, and you can see it.

"He's kissing his badge to the fans. The fans absolutely love him, and it's right that he does leave at the end of the season.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from Manchester United’s match against Aston Villa in the Premier League.

"His legs at times have let him down. What I would say is there's a period in the middle of his four-year contract where he looks heavier in his body and I don't know what happened there.

"Whether it's the training, whether it's something that's happened with him or whatever, I don't know. But he looks fitter, he looks leaner, he looks freer and look he's doing a great job."

Casemiro still a 'poor investment'

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Jamie Carragher admitted he was wrong about Casemiro after claiming the 'football had left him' two years ago earlier this season.

Despite his role in United's push for Champions League qualification and other highlights including his goal in the Carabao Cup final victory over Newcastle in 2023, Neville believes that the holding midfielder has still been a poor investment.

Under Erik ten Hag, United paid an initial £60m plus £10m in add-ons for the Brazilian, who remains one of the highest-earners at the club.

"This is nothing to do with Casemiro, this is to do with Man United's poor recruitment over many, many years and them overpaying," Neville added.

"They've invested £140m in Casemiro, £20m a year in wages and £60m in a fee, that's not a clever move because they're now losing him for nothing.

"If you're spending £140m on a midfield player over four years, you'd expect that there'd be some sort of residual value at the end of it. You wouldn't be expecting that he would be running his career down. It's not a deal they would do now.

"You can't say it's been a great investment at £140m, but that's not Casemiro's fault."

Casemiro has not lost golden touch

Image: Michael Carrick and Casemiro celebrate Man Utd's win over Aston Villa

Analysis by Sky Sports' Laura Hunter:

On the day that Bruno Fernandes closed in on the Premier League's assists record in victory over Villa, Michael Carrick was asked as many questions about the contribution of outgoing Casemiro as he was about his captain.

The Brazilian was another, alongside Fernandes, to lead by example. Finding himself as the most advanced midfielder for much of the game, his four touches in the opposition box were second only to Amad (seven) among the United contingent, while contesting more duels (12) than any other.
